steps.title: Connect history to the public
steps.content: The passage argues that public understanding of history is central to understanding politics and to making an electorate function. Because historical issues affect democratic life, they cannot remain only in the dominion of academics. The blank should therefore point to history as something that must be available beyond specialists, not something reserved for historians alone.

steps.title: Choose the public-facing word
steps.content: A, respectable, gives history a positive status but no reason to move it outside academic control. B, complex, points toward difficulty and would make historians seem more necessary, not less. D, critical, emphasizes importance, but the sentence is focused on why history should not be kept within one scholarly group. C, accessible, fits the idea that history should be open to the public because voters need it to understand politics. The answer is C.
